mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 150k jobs per month signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with PAYEMS > 150k are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts indicates expanding demand for goods and services, creating positive momentum in equity markets over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between INDPRO and HOUST shows Rank IC < 0.03 or 60-day SPX returns are negative when both series rise.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production below 95 signals manufacturing contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during periods of supply-side disruption.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive when INDPRO < 95 or Rank IC < 0.02 in low INDPRO regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% combined with rising interest rates creates a 'stagflation' risk premium that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction between CPIAUCSL > 2.5 and FEDFUNDS > 2.0 fails to predict negative SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03 or p > 0.05 in t-test).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit availability (RSAFS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates strong household financial health, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint effect of RSAFS > 460k and UNRATE < 5.0 fails to predict positive SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03 or p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate below 4.5% signals labor market tightness that increases wage pressures and corporate margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are not significantly positive when UNRATE < 4.5 (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below 98 combined with rising oil prices (not in catalog) would signal supply constraints, but since oil isn't available, we use INDPRO alone as a proxy for supply-side stress.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive when INDPRO < 98 or Rank IC < 0.03 in low INDPRO regimes.
